Title: Patient photo on dashboard exposes meaningful text alternative
Preconditions: Logged-in clinician; patient with an uploaded photo on the dashboard
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard for a patient with a photo | 2. Inspect the patient image element with a screen reader and DOM inspector | 3. Read the accessible name announced
Expected_Result: Image exposes a non-empty alt attribute conveying purpose (e.g. patient name/identity), not the file name or empty alt; screen reader announces it

Title: Decorative iconography is hidden from assistive technology
Preconditions: Logged-in user on any list view containing decorative icons
Test_Steps: 1. Open a screen with decorative-only icons (e.g. spacer/separator glyphs) | 2. Inspect each decorative icon node | 3. Verify it is hidden from AT
Expected_Result: Decorative icons use empty alt='' or aria-hidden=true and are not announced by screen readers

Title: Informational status icon conveys state via text not color/shape alone
Preconditions: Order list with icons representing active/completed/cancelled states
Test_Steps: 1. Open the orders list showing status icons | 2. Inspect each status icon's accessible name | 3. Cross-check icon state against announced text
Expected_Result: Each status icon exposes a text alternative matching its state; no state is conveyed by icon shape/color only

Title: Chart/graph image provides equivalent data text alternative
Preconditions: Patient with vitals trend graph rendered as image/canvas
Test_Steps: 1. Open the vitals graph widget | 2. Inspect the graph container for text alternative or adjacent data table | 3. Verify equivalent information is available to AT
Expected_Result: Graph exposes a text alternative or an accessible data table conveying the same trend information

Title: Negative: image button with no accessible name fails
Preconditions: Any toolbar with image-only buttons
Test_Steps: 1. Identify image-only action buttons (e.g. print, export) | 2. Inspect each for alt/aria-label/title | 3. Flag any with empty accessible name
Expected_Result: Defect raised if any image-only control has no accessible name; control is unusable to screen reader users

Title: CAPTCHA or barcode image offers text/audio alternative
Preconditions: A screen presenting a non-text challenge or scannable code
Test_Steps: 1. Open a screen presenting a barcode/QR or challenge image | 2. Inspect for descriptive text alternative | 3. Confirm purpose is conveyed without forcing visual recognition
Expected_Result: Non-text content has a text alternative describing its purpose; no security info leaked beyond intended use

Title: Registration form fields are programmatically associated with labels
Preconditions: Registration form open
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient registration form | 2. For each input inspect for/id or aria-labelledby association | 3. Verify clicking label focuses the field
Expected_Result: Every input has a programmatically associated label; label click moves focus to its control

Title: Data table uses proper header cells and scope
Preconditions: A clinical data table (e.g. encounters list) is displayed
Test_Steps: 1. Open a tabular list view | 2. Inspect that column/row headers use th with scope | 3. Navigate the table with a screen reader by cell
Expected_Result: Headers use th with correct scope (col/row); screen reader announces row/column headers per cell

Title: Required fields are conveyed programmatically not only visually
Preconditions: Form with required fields marked with asterisk
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect required fields for aria-required or required attribute | 2. Verify the required state is announced | 3. Confirm asterisk meaning is also in text
Expected_Result: Required state is exposed via aria-required/required; not conveyed by red asterisk color alone

Title: Grouped radio/checkbox controls use fieldset/legend or group role
Preconditions: Form containing a grouped choice (e.g. Gender radios)
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect grouped controls for fieldset/legend or role=group with label | 2. Navigate with screen reader | 3. Verify group purpose is announced
Expected_Result: Radio/checkbox group is wrapped in fieldset/legend or labeled group; the group purpose is announced once

Title: Heading structure is hierarchical with no skipped levels
Preconditions: Patient dashboard loaded
Test_Steps: 1. Extract heading outline (h1..h6) of the page | 2. Verify a single h1 and logical nesting | 3. Flag any skipped heading levels
Expected_Result: Headings form a logical hierarchy; no level is skipped (e.g. h2 to h4) and exactly one h1 exists

Title: Reading/DOM order matches visual order for multi-column layout
Preconditions: A multi-column dashboard layout
Test_Steps: 1. Disable CSS positioning or read DOM source order | 2. Compare to visual reading order | 3. Verify meaning is preserved
Expected_Result: DOM order yields a meaningful sequence matching the intended visual reading order

Title: Body text meets 4.5:1 contrast against background
Preconditions: Any text-heavy page loaded
Test_Steps: 1. Sample primary body text foreground/background colors | 2. Compute contrast ratio | 3. Compare against 4.5:1 threshold
Expected_Result: Normal text contrast ratio is at least 4.5:1; boundary values just below fail

Title: Large text meets 3:1 contrast threshold
Preconditions: Page with large headings (>=18.66px bold or >=24px)
Test_Steps: 1. Identify large-text elements | 2. Sample colors and compute ratio | 3. Compare against 3:1 threshold
Expected_Result: Large text contrast ratio is at least 3:1; values below the boundary are flagged

Title: Placeholder-as-label text meets contrast and persistence
Preconditions: Form using placeholder text in inputs
Test_Steps: 1. Sample placeholder text contrast | 2. Verify ratio meets 4.5:1 | 3. Confirm placeholder is not the only label
Expected_Result: Placeholder text meets 4.5:1 and is not relied upon as the sole label; defect if low contrast

Title: Disabled and error text contrast evaluated across states
Preconditions: Form supporting normal/disabled/error states
Test_Steps: 1. Capture text colors in normal, disabled, and error states | 2. Compute contrast per state | 3. Apply pass/fail per WCAG (disabled exempt, error not)
Expected_Result: Error and normal text meet 4.5:1; disabled controls are exempt but evaluated and documented

Title: Content reflows at 320 CSS px without horizontal scroll
Preconditions: Patient dashboard accessible in a resizable browser
Test_Steps: 1. Set viewport to 320 CSS px width (or 400% zoom at 1280px) | 2. Load the dashboard | 3. Scan for two-dimensional scrolling
Expected_Result: Content reflows to single column; no horizontal scrolling required except for data tables/maps exceptions

Title: Wide data table allows scrolling exception without content loss
Preconditions: A wide data table is shown at narrow viewport
Test_Steps: 1. Reduce viewport to 320px with a wide table present | 2. Verify table allows horizontal scroll as permitted exception | 3. Confirm no other content is lost or clipped
Expected_Result: Wide table may scroll horizontally as an allowed exception; surrounding content reflows without loss

Title: Sticky header/footer does not obscure content at 400% zoom
Preconditions: Page with sticky header at high zoom
Test_Steps: 1. Zoom to 400% | 2. Scroll through content | 3. Verify sticky elements do not hide form fields or actions
Expected_Result: Sticky elements do not permanently obscure interactive content; all fields remain reachable

Title: Form input borders meet 3:1 against adjacent colors
Preconditions: Form with bordered inputs
Test_Steps: 1. Sample input border color vs adjacent background | 2. Compute contrast | 3. Compare against 3:1
Expected_Result: Input boundaries have at least 3:1 contrast so field extent is perceivable

Title: Focus indicator color meets 3:1 non-text contrast
Preconditions: Keyboard focus visible on controls
Test_Steps: 1. Tab to a control | 2. Sample focus indicator vs unfocused/background | 3. Compute contrast against 3:1
Expected_Result: Focus indicator has at least 3:1 contrast against adjacent colors

Title: Toggle/checkbox graphical state meets 3:1 contrast
Preconditions: Controls with on/off graphical states
Test_Steps: 1. Capture checkbox/toggle in checked and unchecked states | 2. Sample the indicator graphics | 3. Verify each state meets 3:1
Expected_Result: Graphical control states meet 3:1 so checked/unchecked is distinguishable without relying on color

Title: Chart series rely on more than color and meet contrast
Preconditions: Multi-series clinical chart displayed
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect chart series differentiation | 2. Verify patterns/labels beyond color | 3. Sample series-vs-background contrast
Expected_Result: Series are distinguishable by pattern/label not color alone, and meaningful graphics meet 3:1

Title: Full registration flow operable by keyboard only
Preconditions: Registration accessible to logged-in registrar
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to registration using keyboard only | 2. Complete all fields via Tab/typing/arrow keys | 3. Submit the form using keyboard
Expected_Result: Entire flow is completable with keyboard only; no step requires a pointer

Title: Custom dropdown/autocomplete operable via keyboard
Preconditions: A typeahead control (e.g. concept search) present
Test_Steps: 1. Focus the autocomplete with Tab | 2. Type to filter and use arrow keys to navigate options | 3. Select with Enter and dismiss with Escape
Expected_Result: Options open, navigate, select, and dismiss using keyboard alone with correct value committed

Title: Negative: no keyboard trap in modal or widget
Preconditions: A modal dialog can be opened
Test_Steps: 1. Open a modal with keyboard | 2. Tab through all elements | 3. Verify focus can leave via Tab/Shift+Tab/Escape without trapping
Expected_Result: Focus is never trapped; user can move out of the component using standard keys

Title: Drag-only interactions provide keyboard alternative
Preconditions: A UI with drag-reorder or slider
Test_Steps: 1. Identify any drag-based interaction | 2. Attempt the same operation via keyboard | 3. Verify an equivalent keyboard path exists
Expected_Result: Drag operations have keyboard-operable equivalents; defect if action is pointer-only

Title: Date picker fully operable by keyboard
Preconditions: A date field with a calendar popup
Test_Steps: 1. Focus the date field | 2. Open the picker via keyboard | 3. Navigate months/days with arrows and select with Enter across browser/OS pairs
Expected_Result: Date can be entered and selected by keyboard across tested browser/OS pairings

Title: Tab order follows logical sequence on registration form
Preconditions: Registration form open
Test_Steps: 1. Place focus at top of form | 2. Press Tab repeatedly recording focus order | 3. Compare order to visual/logical sequence
Expected_Result: Focus moves in a logical, meaningful order that preserves operability and meaning

Title: Modal open moves focus into dialog and restores on close
Preconditions: A modal triggered from a button
Test_Steps: 1. Activate the trigger | 2. Verify focus moves into the modal | 3. Close the modal and verify focus returns to the trigger
Expected_Result: On open focus enters dialog; on close focus returns to the invoking control

Title: Negative: positive tabindex values do not disrupt order
Preconditions: Page audited for tabindex usage
Test_Steps: 1. Scan DOM for tabindex>0 | 2. Tab through page | 3. Verify no jarring jumps caused by manual tabindex
Expected_Result: No tabindex>0 disrupts natural order; sequence remains logical

Title: Skip-to-content link is first focusable and functional
Preconditions: Any page with primary navigation
Test_Steps: 1. Load page and press Tab once | 2. Verify a skip link receives focus | 3. Activate it and confirm focus jumps to main content
Expected_Result: Skip link is the first focusable element and moves focus to main content when activated

Title: Visible focus indicator present on all interactive controls
Preconditions: Page with links, buttons, and inputs
Test_Steps: 1. Tab through each interactive control | 2. Observe focus indicator presence | 3. Flag any control with no visible focus
Expected_Result: Every focusable control shows a clearly visible focus indicator; none rely on default removal

Title: Negative: outline:none without replacement is a defect
Preconditions: CSS audit for focus suppression
Test_Steps: 1. Search styles for outline:none | 2. Tab to affected controls | 3. Verify a replacement focus style exists
Expected_Result: Any outline removal is paired with a visible alternative; otherwise defect raised

Title: Focus visible on custom widget options during navigation
Preconditions: A listbox/menu widget present
Test_Steps: 1. Open the widget | 2. Arrow through options | 3. Verify the active option shows a visible focus/selection state at each step
Expected_Result: Active option is visibly indicated at every navigation step; focus never disappears

Title: Validation errors identify the field and describe the problem in text
Preconditions: Registration form open
Test_Steps: 1. Submit form with a missing required field | 2. Observe error messaging | 3. Verify error identifies field and describes the issue in text
Expected_Result: Error is identified in text, names the field, and is programmatically associated; not color-only

Title: Invalid date format produces a specific actionable error
Preconditions: Form with a date field
Test_Steps: 1. Enter an invalid/boundary date (e.g. 31/02/2026 or future DOB) | 2. Submit | 3. Read the error text and association
Expected_Result: Specific error explains the constraint and is tied to the date field via aria-describedby

Title: Error summary links navigate to offending fields
Preconditions: Form returning multiple errors
Test_Steps: 1. Submit form with multiple errors | 2. Verify an error summary lists each error | 3. Activate a summary link and confirm focus moves to the field
Expected_Result: Summary lists all errors; activating a link moves focus to the corresponding field

Title: Negative: error conveyed by red border only fails
Preconditions: Form with color-based error styling
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger a validation error | 2. View under grayscale/simulated color blindness | 3. Verify a text error exists beyond color
Expected_Result: Error remains identifiable without color; defect if only a red border indicates the error

Title: All inputs have visible labels or accessible names
Preconditions: Any data entry form open
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect each input for a visible label or accessible name | 2. Verify instructions for formatted fields | 3. Flag any unlabeled input
Expected_Result: Every input has a persistent visible label or accessible name and any required format instruction

Title: Format hints provided before input is needed
Preconditions: Form field with specific format requirement
Test_Steps: 1. Locate a field needing a format (e.g. phone, identifier) | 2. Verify hint text is present before entry | 3. Confirm hint is associated to the field
Expected_Result: Format instruction is shown before/at the field and programmatically associated via aria-describedby

Title: Label persists when value entered (no disappearing placeholder label)
Preconditions: Form using floating/placeholder labels
Test_Steps: 1. Focus a field that uses placeholder as label | 2. Type a value | 3. Verify the label remains visible/accessible
Expected_Result: Label remains perceivable after entry; placeholder-only labels that vanish are a defect

Title: Custom toggle exposes correct role and state to AT
Preconditions: A custom toggle/switch control present
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect the control for role (switch/checkbox) | 2. Toggle it | 3. Verify name, role, and aria-checked/pressed update
Expected_Result: Control exposes correct role, accessible name, and current value/state that updates on change

Title: Custom combobox exposes ARIA combobox pattern correctly
Preconditions: A custom autocomplete/combobox present
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect for role=combobox, aria-expanded, aria-controls, aria-activedescendant | 2. Open/close and select | 3. Verify states update per interaction
Expected_Result: Combobox roles and states (expanded/collapsed, active option) update correctly across interactions

Title: Modal dialog exposes role=dialog with accessible name
Preconditions: A modal dialog can be opened
Test_Steps: 1. Open the modal | 2. Inspect for role=dialog/alertdialog and aria-modal | 3. Verify aria-labelledby provides the title
Expected_Result: Dialog has role=dialog, aria-modal, and an accessible name from its title

Title: Negative: div-based button lacks role and keyboard semantics
Preconditions: UI containing clickable div/span acting as button
Test_Steps: 1. Identify clickable non-semantic elements | 2. Inspect role and tabindex | 3. Attempt keyboard activation
Expected_Result: Defect if clickable element lacks button role, name, focusability, and Enter/Space activation

Title: Progress/loading control exposes accessible value
Preconditions: A long-running action with progress UI
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger an action with progress indicator | 2. Inspect role=progressbar and aria-valuenow/min/max | 3. Verify values update as progress changes
Expected_Result: Progress control exposes role and current/min/max values that update through the lifecycle

Title: Save confirmation announced via live region without focus change
Preconditions: Editable form that shows a success toast
Test_Steps: 1. Submit a valid change | 2. Listen with screen reader | 3. Verify the success message is announced via aria-live without moving focus
Expected_Result: Success status is announced via role=status/aria-live polite without stealing focus

Title: Error alert announced assertively
Preconditions: Form that can produce a submission error
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger a submit error (e.g. server validation) | 2. Listen with screen reader | 3. Verify the error is announced assertively via role=alert
Expected_Result: Critical error is announced immediately via role=alert/aria-live assertive

Title: Search result count change announced to AT
Preconditions: A live-filtering search present
Test_Steps: 1. Perform a search that updates result count | 2. Listen for an announcement | 3. Verify the result count is conveyed via live region
Expected_Result: Updated result count is announced via a polite live region without requiring focus move

Title: Negative: status conveyed only by visual toast not announced
Preconditions: A toast/notification system
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger a status toast | 2. Verify presence of aria-live container | 3. Confirm AT announces it
Expected_Result: Defect if visual-only toast has no live region and is silent to screen reader users

Title: FHIR-driven status flags surface accessible state in UI
Preconditions: UI rendering a FHIR resource status (e.g. Condition.clinicalStatus)
Test_Steps: 1. Load a resource with a coded status | 2. Inspect the rendered status control/text | 3. Verify role/value and text alternative reflect the code
Expected_Result: Coded FHIR status renders with an accessible name/value matching the code, not color-only

Title: FHIR Media/DocumentReference rendering exposes alternative text
Preconditions: UI displaying a FHIR Media or DocumentReference attachment
Test_Steps: 1. Render an attachment with title/contentType | 2. Inspect the displayed element accessible name | 3. Verify title is used as alt where image
Expected_Result: Attachment title populates the accessible name; missing title is flagged as a defect

Title: FHIR OperationOutcome diagnostics surfaced as accessible error
Preconditions: Client surfaces FHIR validation errors to the user
Test_Steps: 1. Submit a request returning OperationOutcome with issue.diagnostics | 2. Verify the diagnostics text is shown | 3. Confirm it is announced via role=alert
Expected_Result: OperationOutcome diagnostics are rendered as accessible, announced error text linked to context

Title: HL7-sourced result table preserves header relationships
Preconditions: Lab results imported via HL7 ORU rendered in a table
Test_Steps: 1. Render results parsed from an ORU^R01 message | 2. Inspect table headers and units columns | 3. Verify th/scope relationships are intact
Expected_Result: HL7-derived result grid uses proper header cells so screen readers announce test/unit/value relationships

Title: Accessibility tree snapshot validated via DOM/AT API
Preconditions: Test harness can read the accessibility tree
Test_Steps: 1. Capture accessibility tree for a form | 2. Compare names/roles/values to expected baseline | 3. Diff against stored snapshot
Expected_Result: Accessibility tree matches the approved baseline; regressions in name/role/value are detected

Title: Platform notification center messages exposed to AT
Preconditions: Portable platform notification widget present
Test_Steps: 1. Generate a notification | 2. Open the notification center | 3. Verify items have accessible names and unread state is conveyed non-visually
Expected_Result: Notifications expose accessible names and unread/read state via text/ARIA, not color alone

Title: Telemedicine call controls operable by keyboard
Preconditions: A telehealth session UI is available
Test_Steps: 1. Join a session via keyboard | 2. Tab to mute/camera/end controls | 3. Operate each via keyboard
Expected_Result: Mute, camera, and end-call controls are reachable and operable by keyboard with accessible names

Title: Theme variants evaluated for contrast across light/dark/high-contrast
Preconditions: Application supports multiple themes
Test_Steps: 1. Switch among light, dark, and high-contrast themes | 2. Sample key text/control colors per theme | 3. Verify each combination meets thresholds
Expected_Result: All supported theme combinations meet contrast thresholds; failing pairs are documented

Title: Security: ARIA does not leak PHI to AT beyond visible content
Preconditions: Forms with aria-label/aria-describedby on PHI fields
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect ARIA attributes on PHI-bearing controls | 2. Verify no extra PHI is embedded beyond what is visible | 3. Confirm masked fields are not exposed in accessible name
Expected_Result: Accessible names/descriptions do not expose masked or hidden PHI beyond intended visible content

Title: API: rendered control reflects coded value from REST response
Preconditions: Client renders controls from OpenMRS REST data
Test_Steps: 1. Fetch a resource via REST that drives a control's value | 2. Render the control | 3. Verify accessible value matches the API value
Expected_Result: Control accessible value matches the coded value returned by the API; mismatches flagged

Title: Pairwise focus order across browser and screen reader combinations
Preconditions: Multiple browser/AT combinations available
Test_Steps: 1. Tab through a key form in each browser/AT pair | 2. Record focus order | 3. Compare consistency across pairs
Expected_Result: Focus order remains logical and consistent across tested browser and screen reader pairings

Title: Exploratory reflow stress across nested modals and tables at 320px
Preconditions: Complex screen with nested modal and table
Test_Steps: 1. Open a modal containing a table at 320px | 2. Explore scrolling and overflow | 3. Probe for clipped content or lost actions
Expected_Result: No essential content or action is lost; only permitted table scrolling occurs; issues logged

Title: Usability: clinician completes labeled allergy entry under screen reader
Preconditions: Screen reader user with clinician role; allergy form
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to allergy entry with screen reader | 2. Complete coded allergen, reaction, severity via labels | 3. Save and confirm announcement
Expected_Result: Clinician completes the labeled form entirely via AT and receives an announced save confirmation
